focal_character_timelines.selection_reason: Aligned with the movie's ICRP character set.
focal_character_timelines.task3_relevance: Directly aligned with the same key character used in ICRP.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Paul and guards discuss Coffey's arrival; Percy's misconduct angers them. Harry gives Paul Coffey's file, warning it will horrify him, while they note Coffey's meekness despite his size.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: New death row inmate
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Asks Paul about lights due to fear of the dark and shakes hands with him, showing polite behavior.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: To have light at night and interact politely with the guards.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Locked in his cell after shaking hands with Paul.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: The nature of his crime, hinted to be horrifying, and his fear of the dark.
evidence_quotes: COFFEY Do you leave a light on after bedtime?
COFFEY Because I get a little scared in the dark sometimes.
he surprises everybody by offering Paul his hand
Coffey stands a moment as if unsure what to do, then sinks onto the cot with his hands clasped between his knees.

relation_updates: Forms initial trust with Paul Edgecomb
Experiences hostility from Percy Wetmore
auxiliary.persona_anchor: A large, meek man with childlike fear of the dark.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Paul questions why Percy remains despite his transfer application, believing Percy wants to witness an execution; Hal acknowledges this, hoping his departure will follow, while Paul expresses concern for Hal's wife.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Arrested suspect in the murder of the Detterick twins
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Found on the riverbank holding the dead twins and arrested by Deputy McGee
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: In custody under arrest for murder
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Whether he committed the murder
evidence_quotes: John Coffey sits on the riverbank in bloody overalls, his huge feet splayed out before him.
COFFEY I couldn't help it. I tried to take it back, but it was too late.
McGEE (pause) Boy, you are under arrest for murder.

relation_updates: Accused of murdering Detterick twins
Arrested by Deputy Rob McGee
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Large man grieving with the bodies of the twins

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Coffey heals Paul, who walks pain-free and urinates without discomfort, leaving both Paul and Del stunned by the sudden change.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Prisoner who heals through touch
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Heals Paul's groin infection by touching him, demonstrating a healing ability.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: To help Paul by healing his pain
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Physically distressed after healing, gagging and sitting on his bunk
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: The physical toll healing takes on Coffey
evidence_quotes: COFFEY Just to help.
and something goes WHUMP through Paul's body.

relation_updates: Establishes trust with Paul through healing
Paul becomes protective
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Empathetic healer who suffers physically after using his power

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Coffey shares cornbread with Del and Mr. Jingles but keeps the rest after Billy demands a share; Paul warns Billy for disrespect, who spits at him, provoking tension.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Inmate who receives and shares cornbread
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Receives cornbread from Paul, shares a portion with Del and Mr. Jingles, and keeps the remainder after Billy demands some.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Has shared cornbread with Del and Mr. Jingles and is eating the rest.
evidence_quotes: COFFEY Here's for Del and Mr. Jingles then.
COFFEY Well. Fine. I think I'll keep the rest, then.

relation_updates: Strengthens bond with Delacroix
Shows respect to Paul Edgecomb
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Generous in sharing food but asserts his own claim when challenged.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Paul secures Percy's compliance through a handshake deal, then helps him out of the electric chair; the scene shifts to the execution chamber the next night as witnesses arrive and a storm approaches.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Healer of animals
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Coffey heals the injured mouse Mr. Jingles by cupping it, inhaling sharply, and exhaling a cloud of black insects, reviving it in front of Paul, Brutal, Del, Harry, and Dean.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Mr. Jingles is alive and active, running from Coffey's hand.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: The witnesses are stunned and unsure how to interpret Coffey's ability.
evidence_quotes: Coffey puts his mouth to his cupped hands, inhales sharply.
The tail is no longer weak and dying. It's snapping briskly back and forth, as if ready to play.
Dean turns back to Coffey with a stunned whisper: DEAN What did you do?
COFFEY I helped Del's mouse. He a circus mouse.

relation_updates: Guards witness his power
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Compassionate healer

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Delacroix dies after a violent execution; Percy extinguishes the fire, Hal calms the crowd, and Paul confirms death with a stethoscope as chaos subsides.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Empathic inmate
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Physically reacts with shaking and screaming during Delacroix's execution, as if feeling Del's pain.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Distressed and mirroring Delacroix's suffering through physical agitation.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: The ongoing impact of empathetic distress on Coffey.
evidence_quotes: Coffey's shaking and screaming too, as if feeling Del's pain.

auxiliary.persona_anchor: Individual who experiences others' pain in a physical, mirrored response.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Billy dies after drinking the RC cola; Paul, Brutal, Harry, and Dean prepare to proceed, with Coffey unexpectedly volunteering, surprising them all.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Volunteer participant
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Coffey volunteers to join the guards' escape plan after Billy's death.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: To take part in the planned ride
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Included in the escape plan by the guards
evidence_quotes: COFFEY Sure. I'd like to take a ride.
Their heads come slowly around, staring at Coffey in shock.

relation_updates: Cooperative bond with guards strengthened
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Willing to assist when asked

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Melinda, healed and tumor-free, thanks Coffey by giving him her St. Christopher necklace, embracing him tightly as he accepts it, while Paul and Hal look on.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Miraculous healer
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Heals Melinda by inhaling her illness through a kiss, then immediately suffers violent coughing.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Melinda's face restored and tumor healed; Coffey coughing violently and on his knees.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Severe physical deterioration after healing, indicated by persistent coughing.
evidence_quotes: Coffey keeps kissing Melinda in that deep and mysterious way, inhaling and inhaling, her hand held in his like a tiny white bird.
Coffey raises his head, revealing: Melinda's beautiful face.
Coffey regards her raptly for a moment or two, then starts coughing violently.
He turns away and drops to his knees, hacking like a man in the last stages of tuberculosis.

relation_updates: Forms bond with Melinda through healing
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Self-sacrificing healer who endures personal cost to aid others.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Paul experiences Wild Bill's violent murder of the Detterick family, witnessing how he exploited the girls' love to silence them, leaving Paul traumatized and Coffey mourning the world's cruelty.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Prisoner who transfers visions and justice through physical contact
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Coffey grabs Percy through the bars, transfers black 'insects' into him, causing Percy to shoot Wild Bill dead; then offers Paul his hand to show what he saw in Wild Bill's heart.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: To punish Wild Bill for what he did to the Detterick girls and show Paul the truth
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Sitting on the floor at his bars, reaching toward Paul to share a vision
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Paul has not yet taken Coffey's hand to see the vision
evidence_quotes: a massive black arm grabs him through the bars.
COFFEY Punished them bad men.
I saw in his heart. When he grab my arm, I saw what Wild Billy done. Saw plain as day. Can't hide what's in your heart.
Take my hand, boss. You see for yourself.

relation_updates: Shares vision with Paul Edgecomb
auxiliary.persona_anchor: A man who can see what's in people's hearts and uses his power to punish evil

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Coffey, accepting execution, expresses exhaustion from loneliness and human cruelty, and wishes to see a movie before dying, finding brief wonder in its projection.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Condemned inmate who declines an offer to escape
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Paul offers to let him escape, but Coffey refuses, stating he is tired of loneliness and human cruelty and wants the execution to proceed.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: To have his execution carried out to end his suffering
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Accepts execution and requests to see a movie before dying
evidence_quotes: COFFEY Why would you do such a foolish thing?
You tell God the Father it was a kindness you done.

relation_updates: Paul offers escape but Coffey declines
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Tired of human cruelty and loneliness

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Coffey is executed amid emotional tension; Paul hesitates, they share a final connection, Coffey's last thought reveals insight, and he dies peacefully, tears falling as lights burst and witnesses react.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Executed prisoner
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Faces execution after sharing a comforting dream and leading a final prayer with Paul and the guards.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Deceased
evidence_quotes: COFFEY I be all right, fellas. This here's the hard part. I be all right in a little while.
I dreamed those two little blonde-headed girls were there. They 'us laughing, too.
COFFEY ...be with me until the end. Amen.
Coffey closes his eyes, frowning in deep concentration.

relation_updates: Final bond with Paul and guards
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Calm and reassuring in his final moments

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Paul reveals he and Mr. Jingles were infused with life by John Coffey's power; he is 105, has outlived loved ones, and sees immortality as a curse, foreshadowing Elaine's death.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Character whose power infuses life into others
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Paul reveals that John Coffey's power transferred life to him and Mr. Jingles, resulting in Paul's extended age and immunity to illness.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Paul and Mr. Jingles continue to be affected by the life-infusing power, with Paul living to 105 years old.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Paul endures the burden of outliving loved ones and foreseeing Elaine's death due to the immortality.
evidence_quotes: John had to give me a little part of himself...a gift, like...so I could see for myself what Wild Bill had done.
When John did that, a part of whatever power worked through him spilled into me.
He infected us both, didn't he, Mr. Jingles. With life.
I'm a hundred and five years old, Elaine. I was forty the year John Coffey walked the Green Mile.

relation_updates: Spiritual bond with Paul Edgecomb
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Possessor of supernatural power that transfers life to others

focal_character_timelines.timeline_summary: John Coffey arrives on death row as a gentle giant accused of murdering twin girls. He reveals supernatural healing abilities, curing Paul's infection and later Melinda Moores, while suffering deep empathy for others' pain. After using his power to punish the true murderer, Wild Bill, he chooses execution, exhausted by the world's cruelty, and leaves a legacy of immortality for Paul.

focal_character_timelines.selection_reason: Aligned with the movie's ICRP character set.
focal_character_timelines.task3_relevance: Directly aligned with the same key character used in ICRP.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Paul quietly takes a red rain poncho from the wall and escapes unnoticed through the back door.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: escapee
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Paul takes a red rain poncho from the wall and slips out the back door unnoticed.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Paul is outside after successfully escaping.
evidence_quotes: Paul slips to the back door unnoticed.
He helps himself to one
and eases outside, making good his escape.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Paul recalls 1935 on death row, called the Green Mile, where he oversaw executions by electric chair and experienced a severe infection, the year John Coffey arrived.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Paul, a former death row guard, confides in his friend Elaine.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Paul decides to tell Elaine about his past as a death row guard in 1935, including the Green Mile, the electric chair, and John Coffey.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: To share his long-held secret about his time on death row.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Paul begins recounting his experiences on the Green Mile to Elaine.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: The full story of John Coffey and the two dead girls is yet to be told.
evidence_quotes: Paul is staring out the windows, pensive and drained.
PAUL I guess sometimes the past just catches up with you, whether you want it to or not. It's silly.
PAUL I haven't spoken of these things in a long time, Ellie. Over sixty years.
PAUL They usually call death row the Last Mile, but we called ours the Green Mile, because the floor was the color of faded limes.

relation_updates: Paul and Elaine's friendship deepens through this disclosure
status_updates: Paul is now a former death row guard with a traumatic past
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Paul is pensive and burdened, but willing to confide in Elaine after over sixty years of silence.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Paul and guards discuss Coffey's arrival; Percy's misconduct angers them. Harry gives Paul Coffey's file, warning it will horrify him, while they note Coffey's meekness despite his size.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: head guard managing intake and conflicts
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Paul reprimands Percy for misconduct during Coffey's intake and establishes behavioral rules with Coffey.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Percy is removed from the block, and Coffey is settled in his cell with rules explained.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Percy's resentment and potential for further misconduct.
evidence_quotes: PAUL Percy, that's enough.
PAUL (looks to Coffey) Am I gonna have trouble with you, big boy?
PAUL Goddamn it, Percy! Get the hell off my block!
PAUL Your time here can be easy or hard, depends on you.

relation_updates: Paul conflicts with Percy over misconduct
Paul and Coffey share a humanizing handshake
status_updates: Paul is actively managing E Block during Coffey's arrival

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Coffey heals Paul, who walks pain-free and urinates without discomfort, leaving both Paul and Del stunned by the sudden change.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: head guard managing crisis aftermath
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Paul is healed of his pain and fever when John Coffey grabs him and touches his groin.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Paul's pain and fever are gone, and he is stunned by the sudden healing.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Paul must process how Coffey healed him and what to do about it.
evidence_quotes: As soon as he's alone, Paul gives in to the pain, holding his crotch and sinking to his knees with a moan.
Fever's gone. So is the pain in his groin.
Paul comes back to the real world, weak against the bars, realizes Del is hollering in his cell:

relation_updates: Paul's trust in Coffey deepens after experiencing his healing power
status_updates: Paul's debilitating pain and fever are completely cured
Paul witnesses Coffey's supernatural healing ability firsthand

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Delacroix dies after a violent execution; Percy extinguishes the fire, Hal calms the crowd, and Paul confirms death with a stethoscope as chaos subsides.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Paul discovers Percy's sabotage during Del's execution
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Paul realizes the sponge is dry, attempts to stop the execution but is restrained by Brutal, and orders the current to continue.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: Paul aims to manage the botched execution by continuing the current to avoid further complications.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Delacroix is being electrocuted under Paul's order as the execution proceeds horrifically.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Paul must handle Percy's deliberate sabotage and the aftermath of the botched execution.
evidence_quotes: Paul responds with a harsh whisper: PAUL It's dry!
PAUL NO! ROLL, FOR CHRIST'S SAKE, ROLL!

relation_updates: Paul's hostility toward Percy intensifies due to the sabotage
status_updates: Paul is overseeing a horrific botched execution

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Paul experiences Wild Bill's violent murder of the Detterick family, witnessing how he exploited the girls' love to silence them, leaving Paul traumatized and Coffey mourning the world's cruelty.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Paul witnesses the truth of the Detterick murders through a psychic vision.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Paul sees Wild Bill threaten the Detterick twins by exploiting their love for each other during the murder.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Paul is gasping and numb, having confirmed Coffey's innocence.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: What to do with the knowledge of Coffey's innocence.
evidence_quotes: Paul is gasping, back in the real world where his men are staring at him with wide eyes.
Paul nods, numb.
He kill 'em with they love.

relation_updates: Paul and Coffey share profound emotional connection through shared knowledge of the truth
status_updates: Paul now knows Wild Bill committed the murders Coffey was accused of

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Coffey is executed amid emotional tension; Paul hesitates, they share a final connection, Coffey's last thought reveals insight, and he dies peacefully, tears falling as lights burst and witnesses react.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Paul orders Coffey's execution after a final telepathic connection
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Paul hesitates to give the order, touches Coffey's hand, hears a whispered thought about love, and then says 'Roll on two'
timeline_nodes.goal_state: To fulfill his duty as warden by ordering the execution
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Paul has given the execution order and Coffey has died peacefully
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Paul's internal conflict over executing Coffey
evidence_quotes: PAUL Roll on two.
COFFEY (whispered V.O.) He kill 'em with they love. That's how it is ever' day. All over the worl'...
Coffey's expression is peaceful, as if sleeping.

relation_updates: Paul shares final telepathic connection with Coffey during execution
status_updates: Paul fulfills his duty as warden by ordering Coffey's execution

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Paul reveals he and Mr. Jingles were infused with life by John Coffey's power; he is 105, has outlived loved ones, and sees immortality as a curse, foreshadowing Elaine's death.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Paul tells Elaine that John Coffey's power made him immortal and 105 years old.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Paul reveals his immortality as a curse, stating he has outlived loved ones and will outlive Elaine.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Elaine is informed of Paul's immortality and its personal cost.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Paul's curse of outliving loved ones, including Elaine.
evidence_quotes: I'm a hundred and five years old, Elaine.
I've had to watch my friends and loved ones die off through the years...
...and you, Elaine. You'd die, too, and my curse is knowing I'll be there to see it...

relation_updates: Deepens trust with Elaine through shared secret
Reinforces spiritual bond with John Coffey

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Paul mourns Elaine's death, viewing it as punishment for allowing John Coffey's execution, and places a rose on her casket.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Paul attends Elaine's funeral and expresses guilt over John Coffey's execution.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Paul mourns Elaine at her casket, stating her death is his punishment for allowing John Coffey's execution.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Paul is left alive and alone after Elaine's death, burdened by his guilt.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Paul's immortality and remorse for executing John Coffey.
evidence_quotes: Paul, dressed in a dark suit, comes up the aisle.
PAUL (V.O.) ...that's my punishment, you see? My punishment for letting John Coffey ride the lightning...for killing a miracle of God...
Paul lays a rose atop the casket.
PAUL (V.O.) ...you'll be gone, like all the others, and I'll have to stay...

relation_updates: Relationship with Elaine ends in death

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Paul reflects on mortality and the long path to death, symbolized by Mr. Jingles' restless sleep, evoking the enduring emotional weight of loss and life's inevitable end.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Paul awake at night, reflecting on loss and his own lifespan
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Paul questions how much longer he has to live, comparing his mortality to Mr. Jingles' long life
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Paul lies awake, troubled by thoughts of death, observing Mr. Jingles sleep
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: The question of how much longer Paul has to live remains unanswered
evidence_quotes: Paul lies awake, staring at the moon outside his window.
if he could make a mouse live so long, how much longer do I have?
He looks over at the nightstand...

relation_updates: Paul reflects on the loss of his wife, Jan

focal_character_timelines.timeline_summary: Paul, a retiree in a nursing home, escapes and confides in Elaine about his past as a death row guard in 1935. In the flashback, he oversees executions, forms a bond with John Coffey, witnesses miracles, and executes Coffey despite knowing his innocence. This leads to Paul's immortality, which he reveals to Elaine before her death, leaving him to reflect on his eternal punishment.

focal_character_timelines.selection_reason: Aligned with the movie's ICRP character set.
focal_character_timelines.task3_relevance: Directly aligned with the same key character used in ICRP.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Paul and guards discuss Coffey's arrival; Percy's misconduct angers them. Harry gives Paul Coffey's file, warning it will horrify him, while they note Coffey's meekness despite his size.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Guard escorting John Coffey and assaulting inmate Delacroix
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Percy announces 'Dead man walking' during Coffey's escort, strikes Coffey with his baton, and breaks Delacroix's fingers after being ordered to leave the block.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Percy is ordered off E Block by Paul and leaves angrily.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Percy's violent outburst and defiance of Paul's authority remain unresolved.
evidence_quotes: Percy keeps yanking on the big man's cuffs, leading him along with a cry of: PERCY Dead man walking! Dead man--
Percy raps him smartly with the tip of his hickory baton to get him moving faster.
Percy swings his baton and smashes Del's fingers with a LOUD CRACK.
Goddamn it, Percy! Get the hell off my block!

relation_updates: Violent hostility established with Delacroix
Open conflict with Paul after assault
status_updates: Removed from E Block by Paul after incident
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Bullies inmates using his guard authority.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Percy demands Brutal's spot for the next execution, threatening to stay permanently if denied; Paul is incredulous but walks away, leaving the matter unresolved.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Guard defying superiors over execution role
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Percy mocks Bitterbuck's corpse, is shoved by Brutal, then demands Brutal's spot for the next execution, threatening to stay if denied.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Paul walks away without responding, leaving Percy's demand unresolved.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Percy's demand for a frontline execution role and his threat to make a career at the prison.
evidence_quotes: Adios, Chief. Drop us a card from hell, let us know if it's hot enough.
Brutal knocks Percy's hand away, shoves him aside.
I want Brutal's spot for the next execution.
I might just stick around for good, make me a career of this.

relation_updates: Adversarial relationship with Paul and Brutal escalates through direct confrontation
status_updates: Ordered to clean up restraint room after causing chaos
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Defiant and provocative, willing to confront superiors to get what he wants.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Percy threatens Delacroix, who stops laughing; Percy leaves as the scene transitions to 'The Bad Death of Eduard Delacroix'.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Guard assaulted and sexually taunted by inmate Wild Bill Wharton
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Percy mocks Delacroix with a fake snarl, then drifts too close to Wild Bill's cell. Bill grabs him, slams him against the bars, strokes his hair, sexually taunts him, and squeezes his crotch. Percy urinates in his pants and is publicly mocked by Delacroix.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Percy cringes against a cell, breathing fast, with a large urine stain visible on his pants, publicly humiliated in front of the other inmates.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Percy's humiliation is witnessed by the entire block; potential for retaliation or further loss of authority.
evidence_quotes: He bares his teeth in a mock snarl and curls his fingers as if to grab Del.
Wild Bill's arms thrust out, grabbing Percy and slamming him back against the bars with an arm around his throat.
Billy strokes Percy's hair and whispers in his ear: BILLY Ain't you sweet. Soft. Like a girl.
he looks down, sees the huge dark stain spreading at his crotch.

relation_updates: Billy assaults and sexually taunts Percy
Delacroix mocks Percy publicly
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Cowardly when physically confronted; collapses under threat rather than fighting back.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Paul secures Percy's compliance through a handshake deal, then helps him out of the electric chair; the scene shifts to the execution chamber the next night as witnesses arrive and a storm approaches.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Guard who deliberately kills an inmate's pet mouse
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Percy stomps on and kills Mr. Jingles, Delacroix's pet mouse, while smiling at the act.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Mr. Jingles is dead, Delacroix is sobbing, Coffey requests the mouse, and guards are stunned and furious.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Guards' anger and potential confrontation with Percy.
evidence_quotes: Percy is drifting up the block, listening too, but nobody's really paying him much mind.
Percy stomps the heel of his heavy work shoe down on Mr. Jingles.
PERCY Knew I'd get him sooner or later. Just a matter of time, really.
Percy strolls past, exits to the execution chamber.

relation_updates: Hostility toward Delacroix intensifies by killing his pet
Antagonism toward guards escalates as they witness the act
status_updates: Commits deliberate act of cruelty that sets off chain of events
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Cruel to those who cannot retaliate

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Paul secures Percy's compliance through a handshake deal, then helps him out of the electric chair; the scene shifts to the execution chamber the next night as witnesses arrive and a storm approaches.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Guard coerced into execution deal
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Percy, pinned by Paul and threatened with exposure for standing by during an assault, agrees to preside over Delacroix's execution and then transfer to Briar Ridge.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: To oversee Delacroix's execution as agreed to secure the transfer.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Percy has shaken hands on the deal and is bound to preside over Delacroix's execution.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Whether Percy will follow through on his promise to oversee the execution.
evidence_quotes: I put you out front for Del, you put in your transfer to Briar Ridge the very next day.
But four men will swear you stood by while Wild Bill tried to strangle Dean to death.
Percy looks from one man to another, knowing he's trapped.
Percy thinks it over, nods.

relation_updates: Bound by forced agreement with Paul and Brutal
status_updates: Transfer to Briar Ridge conditional on execution performance
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Submits to coercion when physically trapped and threatened.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Delacroix dies after a violent execution; Percy extinguishes the fire, Hal calms the crowd, and Paul confirms death with a stethoscope as chaos subsides.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Guard deliberately sabotaging execution by pretending to wet the sponge
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Percy pretends to wet the sponge with brine for the electric chair, setting up a likely botched execution.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Percy has completed the deceptive action, and the execution is about to proceed with a dry sponge.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: The consequences of the sabotage are pending as the execution has not yet occurred.
evidence_quotes: PERCY No such place. That's just a fairy tale these guys told you to keep you quiet. Just thought you should know, faggot.
PERCY takes the sponge and bends down to the bucket of brine. The other don't see it, but we do: Percy only pretends to

relation_updates: Paul and Brutal witness Percy's cruelty and incompetence
Relationship with other guards deteriorates further
status_updates: Status as competent guard destroyed; now seen as cruel and dangerous
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Demonstrates cruelty and a desire to inflict pain through taunts and deliberate sabotage.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: After Delacroix's botched execution due to a dry sponge, Percy blames ignorance; Hal and Paul condemn him, forcing a transfer to Briar Ridge, while Brutal disposes of the body, escalating tensions.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Guard punished for botched execution
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Percy is punched by Brutal, insulted by Hal, and ordered to submit a transfer request to Briar Ridge for the botched execution.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Percy has a bloody lip, is verbally abused, and agrees to transfer to Briar Ridge.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Transfer to Briar Ridge is mandated, but the execution's consequences persist.
evidence_quotes: PERCY I didn't know the sponge was supposed to be wet.
Brutal hauls off and slugs him.
HAL You're a little asshole, and I don't like you a bit.
PAUL He'll be putting in a transfer request to Briar Ridge tomorrow.

relation_updates: Brutal physically attacks Percy
Hal explicitly expresses contempt and orders transfer
status_updates: Forced to leave Cold Mountain for Briar Ridge
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Percy is held accountable and punished by colleagues for his mistake.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Paul experiences Wild Bill's violent murder of the Detterick family, witnessing how he exploited the girls' love to silence them, leaving Paul traumatized and Coffey mourning the world's cruelty.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Guard subjected to Coffey's psychic possession
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Percy is grabbed by Coffey, receives a transfer of black insects, becomes catatonic, and shoots Wild Bill while in this state.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Catatonic and restrained after shooting Wild Bill
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Percy's catatonic state and the consequences of his actions
evidence_quotes: a massive black arm grabs him through the bars.
Percy, however, is ashen gray. His expression is blank as a sheet of paper, not a trace of awareness in his eyes.
and then he pulls his gun and empties it into Wild Bill as fast as he can pull the trigger.

relation_updates: Victimized by Coffey
Becomes murderer of Wild Bill
status_updates: Commits murder while in trance state
auxiliary.persona_anchor: A guard overpowered and controlled by supernatural force

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Percy's final destination is revealed as Briar Ridge Mental Hospital, where he has been transferred, shown through a widening shot from his window view.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Inmate at Briar Ridge Mental Hospital
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Percy is shown at the window of Briar Ridge Mental Hospital, indicating his transfer there.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Located at Briar Ridge Mental Hospital as his final institutional stop.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: None
evidence_quotes: we WIDEN SLOWLY from Percy at the window
BRIAR RIDGE MENTAL HOSPITAL
He finally got that transfer.

auxiliary.persona_anchor: Has been transferred to Briar Ridge Mental Hospital, which is his last stop.

focal_character_timelines.timeline_summary: Percy Wetmore is an abusive, cowardly guard on E Block who uses his political connections to avoid consequences. He escalates from bullying inmates to deliberately botching an execution, leading to a forced transfer agreement. After being supernaturally punished by John Coffey for his cruelty, he becomes catatonic and is permanently institutionalized at Briar Ridge.
